items.title: Ambassador Rae's Remarks to Commemorate the 80th Anniversary of the Signing of the UN Charter
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content: <article>Mr. Bob Rae, President of ECOSOC and Permanent Representative of Canada to the United Nations, addresses the United Nations General Assembly Hall upon the commemoration of the 80th anniversary of the signing of the United Nations Charter, on Thursday, 26 June 2025.</article>

items.title: G7 Leaders' Summit 2025: Summit wrap-up
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>Under Canada’s leadership, the #G7 Leaders’ Summit helped deliver meaningful outcomes for Canadians and our international partners.
 
From G7 discussions on global issues to bilateral meetings with outreach partners, the 2025 Leader’s Summit highlighted how we can work towards a new era of collaboration – one rooted in mutual support and reliable partnerships.
 
The discussions may have ended, but the work is still underway.

#G7Kananaskis2025 #G7Canada</article>

items.title: G7 Leaders' Summit 2025: Day 2 wrap-up
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>Day 2 of the #G7 Leaders’ Summit saw G7 members, outreach countries and invited guests engage in discussions on key priorities.
 
They identified ways to collaborate on energy security and discussed sustainable and innovative solutions to boost energy access and affordability.
 
#G7Kananaskis2025 #G7Canada</article>

items.title: My passport was stolen. What do I do now?
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>Marie-Christine Picard, Youth Ambassador for International Experience Canada, shares her experience of obtaining an emergency passport abroad with the help of our consular officers. 
Find out how to contact the Canadian office closest to your destination: 
https://travel.gc.ca/assistance/embassies-consulates</article>

items.title: Helping Canadians Travellers – International travel advice from a seasoned explorer
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content: <article>Marie-Christine Picard, youth ambassador for International Experience Canada, shares her checklist for a good experience traveling abroad. For smoother travels, make your first stop at travel.gc.ca</article>

items.title: G7 Leaders' Summit 2025: Day 1 wrap-up
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>It was a productive first day of the #G7 Leaders’ Summit, focused on shared goals of delivering concrete and lasting outcomes on global issues.  
#G7Kananaskis2025</article>

items.title: From Classroom to Global Boardroom: G7 Mock Summit
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>What happens when high school students take on the roles of world leaders? 
In this short video, students from Bow Valley, Alberta, talk about their experiences as G7 members during a mock summit, where they debated about global issues that matter to everyone. 
  
Hear directly from two students and a teacher as they reflect on the power of experiential learning. 
  
Learn more about Canada’s 2025 G7 Presidency at https://g7.canada.ca 
#G7Kananaskis2025 #G7Canada #YouthLeadership #MockSummit #StudentVoices</article>

items.title: Mother's Day 2025 - Bring Kids Back
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>Since 2022, thousands of children from Ukraine were illegally deported to Russia.  

Bring them back to their families and communities.  
#BringKidsBack 

https://international.gc.ca/world-monde/issues_development-enjeux_developpement/response_conflict-reponse_conflits/crisis-crises/ukraine-children-enfants.aspx?lang=eng</article>

items.title: What is accreditation?
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content: <article>Summits and other large-scale events rely on a process called accreditation to ensure their success and the safety of all participants.</article>

items.title: The road to Kananaskis series: What is the G7?
items.author: Global Affairs Canada

items.title: What are Tariffs?
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>What are tariffs? 
Tariffs are taxes or duties that countries apply to imports. They don’t appear on sales receipts.  

How do tariffs affect me?  
Tariffs can impact the prices you pay at the store. When the cost of importing goods increases, businesses pass the bulk of this cost onto consumers.  

Why do Canada’s free trade agreements matter?  
Canada’s 15 free trade agreements provide Canadians with tariff-free access to nearly all products traded between Canada and its free-trade partners. This helps Canadian businesses access markets in over 50 countries representing 1.5 billion consumers and keeps us connected to the world. 

For more information: https://www.canada.ca/unitedstatestariffs</article>

items.title: Mind the Gap, please – AI Fairness in Health and the importance of diverse and inclusive data sets
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content: <article>This interview was taken in Vienna on February 11, 2025 on the International Day of Women and Girls in Science at the Austrian Academy of Science at an International Expert Panel Discussion organized by the diplomatic missions of Canada, France, Italy, Sweden, Switzerland, the Netherlands and the United Kingdom.</article>
